evals.prompt: Just got out of a meeting with the ops team. They're frustrated because every time a deployment fails they have to SSH into the server, tail the logs, and figure out what went wrong. Sometimes it takes 30 mins just to find the relevant error. They want us to build an ELK stack so they can search logs from a dashboard. Also mentioned wanting Slack alerts when something goes wrong. Can you turn this into a spec?
evals.expected_output: A concise, solution-agnostic spec (~30 lines) that reframes 'ELK stack' and 'Slack alerts' into outcome-based acceptance criteria about log searchability and failure notifications, with a clear Why, Who (user story), and distinct non-repetitive acceptance criteria.
expectations: Spec contains a Why section explaining the business need
Spec contains a user story in As.../I want.../so that... format
Each acceptance criterion is distinct — no two criteria restate the same requirement in different words
No acceptance criterion prescribes an implementation detail the team would naturally handle without being told
Spec does not prescribe ELK/Elasticsearch/Logstash/Kibana as the solution
Acceptance criteria do not prescribe Slack — alerting framed as an outcome
Acceptance criteria describe observable behaviours, not implementation details
Entire spec is under 60 lines of Markdown

evals.prompt: Our sales team keeps asking for better reporting. They want to see how deals are progressing and where things are getting stuck in the pipeline. Right now they export stuff to Excel and manually build charts. The VP of Sales wants this sorted by end of Q2.
evals.expected_output: A concise, solution-agnostic spec (~30 lines) that extracts the core problem (lack of visibility into deal progression) without prescribing the solution, with distinct outcome-based acceptance criteria.
expectations: Spec contains a Why section explaining the business need
Spec contains a user story in As.../I want.../so that... format
Each acceptance criterion is distinct — no two criteria restate the same requirement in different words
No acceptance criterion prescribes an implementation detail the team would naturally handle without being told
Spec does not prescribe specific technologies
Acceptance criteria address the core need: deal progression and bottleneck identification
Acceptance criteria describe observable behaviours, not implementation details
Entire spec is under 60 lines of Markdown

evals.prompt:
title: 'show customer's full history' | epic: title: customer support web ui

To help improve the experience for customers when they contact customer support we want to build a web portal to help customer support agents quickly find the right information about their users across our large user base. We have a number of internal and third party systems that hold customer data and it would be ideal if we could consolidate the useful information into a single view. The customer support team currently has to switch between 4-5 different tools to find what they need, which slows down call handling times and frustrates both agents and customers.
evals.expected_output: A concise, solution-agnostic spec (~30 lines) that preserves the title/epic context, writes a clear user story for the support agent, and defines distinct acceptance criteria around consolidated view, response time, and information completeness — without naming specific systems or UI frameworks.
expectations: Spec contains a Why section explaining the business need
Spec contains a user story in As.../I want.../so that... format
Each acceptance criterion is distinct — no two criteria restate the same requirement in different words
No acceptance criterion prescribes an implementation detail the team would naturally handle without being told
Spec does not prescribe specific technologies
Spec preserves or references the title/epic context from the input
Acceptance criteria describe observable behaviours, not implementation details
Entire spec is under 60 lines of Markdown
